summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a fire‑filled Pine Knob show, highlighted by blistering Scarlet > Fire and soaring jams on The Other One
review: Fans rave about the high‑quality SBD source that delivers crystal‑clear instrumentation while celebrating a show packed with big second‑set jams – especially the searing Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain, an explosive The Other One, and memorable moments like Victim and Stella Blue.  Jerry’s vocals and guitar work are praised (even his Dire Wolf slip‑up is taken in good humor), Phil’s bass leads the band, Vince’s keyboards shine, and Mickey’s drum antics add extra excitement.

ai_rating.rationale: Over a dozen detailed reviews overwhelmingly positive about both performance and soundboard quality; only one low‑star comment, no serious recording flaws.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Listeners repeatedly note the great SBD copy – clear, well‑balanced mix with no reported dropouts or muddiness.

standout_songs: Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain
show_quality.setlist_flow: Second set is jam‑heavy with seamless transitions; fans highlight extended jams after Victim, Scarlet and Fire, plus a wildly inventive The Other One that kept the energy soaring.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for his vocal delivery (Stella Blue, Dire Wolf) and iconic guitar work; even his lyrical slip on Dire Wolf is noted as humorous.
band_member_comments.Phil: Repeatedly lauded for taking charge and delivering a powerful bass performance throughout the night.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince’s keyboard contributions are highlighted as excellent and heavily laced into the set.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey’s drum antics (crawling on top of the kit) are mentioned as part of the memorable jam moments.
